children.name: Sagittarius
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the singular secretarybird, a distinctive raptor of African savannas that hunts almost entirely on foot. Standing over a metre tall on long, crane-like legs, it is equally at home stalking through tall grass or soaring on thermals, and its unique morphology places it in its own family within Accipitriformes.

children.name: Sagittarius serpentarius
children.description: An unmistakable long-legged raptor with grey body plumage, black flight feathers, and elongated central tail feathers. It dispatches snakes, rodents, and insects with powerful downward kicks of up to 200 newtons of force, earning it a reputation as the serpent-hunter of the African plains.
